---
title: "Preparing DEM Data"
lang: en
execute:
  eval: false
---

::: {.callout-note}
[Japanese version](../../notebooks/dem_download.qmd)
:::

## Download

Download a DEM (digital elevation model) from the Geospatial Information Authority of Japan's Fundamental Geospatial Data Download Service.

- [Fundamental Geospatial Data Download Service](https://service.gsi.go.jp/kiban/)

The coordinate reference system appears to have changed from "JGD2011" to "JGD2024".

Because the data are selected by first-level mesh, it is useful to identify the second-level mesh that covers your target area first.
The following GSI page can help you keep the downloaded data as small as possible.

- [Publication range for Digital Topographic Map second-level mesh sheets | GSI](https://www.gsi.go.jp/kibanjoho/kibanjoho50014.html)

## Convert to tif

Data from the [Fundamental Geospatial Data Download Service](https://service.gsi.go.jp/kiban/) are distributed as zip files.
After extracting them, you will find XML files.

For easier analysis, convert them to GeoTIFF (`.tif`).
The easiest option is to use the QGIS [QuickDEM4JP](https://plugins.qgis.org/plugins/QuickDEM4JP/) plugin.

This plugin makes it straightforward to create a DEM in tif format.

## Crop

If the DEM is too large, crop it to the required extent with the `clip_raster` function from [**whitebox**](https://whiteboxr.gishub.org/index.html).

If you prefer a GUI workflow, [WhiteboxTools GUI](https://jblindsay.github.io/ghrg/whiteboxgui/) is also useful.

You can also crop rasters in QGIS.
